annotation_target.structure: Post-climax descent consequent — resignation, bass far from home
key_moments: m21 bass has traveled far from opening E
teaching_goals: the mood is resignation — not sadness, but acceptance
LH continues its patient chromatic descent
style_tips: even softer than the opening — pp to ppp
the descent is now complete emotionally

teaching_notes.note: The bass has traveled far — its distance from the opening is part of the emotional arc.
technique: listen to the bass journey across the whole piece

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 21–24 of Prelude in E Minor, Op. 28 No. 4 (continuation of mm. 17–20), analyze the musical continuation, play in a loop.

session.content: Play measures 21–24 and describe the continuation of the descent.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 21–24.
Measures 21–24 continue the descent — the music is moving toward resignation. The chromatic bass has traveled far from the opening E and the RH melody settles. Let me loop mm. 21–24.
Chopin Prelude E minor mm. 21–24: descent continues, resignation quality, bass far from opening. Pairs with mm. 17–20.
